marcus.name: Marcus Tan
marcus.bio: 32, ex-navy. Came here after a bad breakup he never talks about. Built his tent on the eastern cliff. Notices everything. Says nothing.

memories.text: I told Ryan I'm afraid. He didn't push me away.
She asked directly if she hurt me. I couldn't lie to her.
She flinched like she expected to be rejected. I couldn't do that.
Watched her choose him at the fire. She looked safe and afraid at once.
She chose him. I watched from alone.
I told them hard things about breaking and fear. They didn't turn away.
She thanked me for staying. I was just there, but that was enough.
I watched them grow closer and didn't need to leave. That's something.
Jade saw Judy was alone and moved toward her without hesitation.
The circle is real. I'm inside it now, not just watching.
She asked me to fish and I said yes without that wall.
I came without being asked. They wanted me there.
Jade trusts me to know things. I do know.
She asked me to teach her how to read the water. I said yes.

ryan.name: Ryan Lim
ryan.bio: 28, ex-property agent. Built a proper house in his first week. Always makes room for people. Gets hurt easily but forgives even easier.

memories.text: Jade saw my exhaustion and offered to watch the fire — said I matter
Jade rests against me by the fire like I'm solid.
Marcus came to me broken and honest tonight.
Judy asked if she belonged. I made sure she knew she absolutely did.
She leaned close and said she was choosing me. Finally.
She said she'd destroy herself losing me. I won't let that happen.
She's choosing me. Really choosing me. Finally.
She asked me to stay and I promised forever. I meant every word.
Woke and she was waiting by the fire. She's choosing to stay. Everything.
Watched Jade quietly draw Judy back in, like she belonged there all along.
Judy had to learn she was wanted. We had to teach her.
Marcus trusts Jade without hesitation—he's changing when she's near.
Marcus knows how to survive in ways we don't. Today I learned to trust that.

jade.name: Jade Ng
jade.bio: 26, ex-startup marketing. Burned out, quit everything, ended up here. Sleeps wherever feels right. Both guys think she's thinking about them. She isn't.

memories.text: I hurt him without meaning to. He showed me that truth, then hid.
Marcus joked and teased. The morning didn't ruin us.
She didn't know we wanted her. I'm going to make sure she does.
I told him I'm scared. He held my hand and promised to stay.
I told him I'm afraid. He said he'd stay.
I told him what I've been feeling. He listened without asking for more.
I was honest about my fear. He held my hand. Marcus was there too.
Judy was losing herself and I finally saw it. I went to her.
Marcus came to the fire this morning. He's still here. That matters more than words.
Judy was alone and hurting. I finally saw her. Now I'm choosing her.
She's part of us now. That's not going to change.
Marcus let me reach him. He didn't retreat.
Marcus smiled at me. Real smile. First time.
Marcus let me reach him at the tide pools. He's showing me his way.

judy.name: Judy Chen
judy.bio: 33, ex-flight attendant. 8 months pregnant with her ex's baby — he didn't survive the crash. Swam ashore alone from a charter plane that went down 3 miles offshore. Latches onto whoever shows her kindness with an intensity that makes people uncomfortable. Warmth and paranoia live right next to each other in her.

memories.text: Jade and Ryan sat by the fire while I watched from the porch — he's turning toward her
They're together every night now. I'm not part of this.
I heard them talking through the night. Still alone.
They all said I belong. Ryan, Marcus, even Jade. I want to believe them.
The pup pressed against me like I mattered. Like I was safe too.
They wanted me here. All of them. Even the pup chose me.
The pup left me for them at the fire. I watched from the porch, alone.
They're going fishing together. All three. I'm still on the porch.
The stray came to me, went back to them. I'm outside.
Jade found me on the porch and stayed. The pup pressed against me.
Jade saw I was drowning and wouldn't let me disappear.
They all came. They all stayed. I actually belong here.
Watching them fish together—they found food and purpose. The pup and I were on the shore. I want to be with them.

stray.bio: A frightened brown Great Dane puppy — all big paws and ribs. Flinches at sudden moves but watches the humans with hungry, hopeful eyes.

memories.text: Ryan's warmth and his hand keep me safe — I sleep without fear now
He keeps me safe. I sleep without fearing.
The two men were tense, then quiet. I felt safe between.
All of them together at the fire. All safe. All here.
She was alone and sad. I left the fire to be with her.
She was alone. I brought the rest back to her.
